title: Use CST timezone for getCurrentShow matching

requirements.text: Update the `getCurrentShow` backend function in `backend/main.mo` to determine the current day of the week and current time using CST (Central Standard Time, UTC-6) instead of UTC. Apply the UTC-6 offset to the IC system time before matching against each show's `scheduleDay` and `scheduleTime` fields so that shows go on air at the correct local CST times.

quotes: make sure the shows go on air cst time zone

acceptanceCriteria: The `getCurrentShow` function converts the current IC system time to CST (UTC-6) before extracting the current day and time.
A show scheduled for, e.g., Monday at 9:00 AM CST becomes active when it is Monday 9:00 AM in the CST timezone, not UTC.
When UTC time crosses a day boundary but CST has not yet, the CST day is used for matching (e.g., Tuesday 1:00 AM UTC is still Monday in CST).
The existing return type and API signature of `getCurrentShow` remain unchanged.

constraints: Do not change the Show or DJ data model schemas.
Do not modify any frontend immutable paths.
nonGoals: Supporting dynamic or user-selectable timezone settings.
Adjusting any frontend display formatting for timezone.
